commit.message:
security: remove unauthenticated global JSON HTTP routes

The raw HTTP endpoints for global JSON had no token requirement
(except /post/global which had token check). They exposed all data
to anyone with network access, bypassing the auth layer entirely.

Removed routes:
  - GET  /get/global         (no auth)
  - GET  /get/global/path    (no auth)
  - POST /post/global        (had token but redundant)
  - POST /post/global/patch  (no auth)
  - POST /post/global/persist (no auth)

All global JSON operations are now only accessible via POST /post/run
with the proper commands (get_global_json, set_global_json,
patch_global_json, persist_global_json) which enforce token auth
for sensitive operations.

Updated README to remove the Global JSON Storage section from API docs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix 5 security issues: module cache rebuild, rent fee lag, close time gating, nomination timeout, Soroban resource enforcement

#1202: Module cache now rebuilds on any Soroban-era protocol change
  (prev != new && new >= 25), not just V25 transition.

#1203: soroban_fee_write_1kb re-read from post-upgrade config after
  apply_upgrades_to_delta, so LedgerCloseMetaExtV1 uses correct value.

#1166: Upgrade gating now uses lcl_close_time instead of candidate
  close_time in compute_candidate_value, extract_valid_value, and
  check_upgrades_valid. Prevents one-ledger-early activation.

#1213: get_upgrade_nomination_timeout_limit() now reads from stored
  UpgradeParameters instead of returning hard-coded u32::MAX.

#1175: Tx-set selection uses 1x ledger-max Soroban resource limits
  (separate from 2x queue-admission limits). Seeded at bootstrap
  and after each ledger close via seed_validation_context().

commit.message:
Phase 2: CPU AddrNet + RwLock trie + proactive prefetch

CPU-resident AddrNet (Phase 2a):
- new cpu_addr.rs: CpuAddrNet (pure Rust MLP, no Candle) + CpuAddrBank (Arc<RwLock>)
- model.rs: addr_nets: Option<Vec<AddrNet>> — None after Phase B finalization
  AddrNet::extract_cpu_weights(), ANT::sync_cpu_addr_bank(), ANT::drop_addr_nets()
- engine.rs: cpu_addr_bank field (always present, zeroed until finalize_phase_b)
  finalize_phase_b(): sync GPU weights → CpuAddrBank, drop GPU addr_nets
  compute_batch_addrs_cpu(): hidden Tensor → CPU → CpuAddrBank forward
  All address paths (read_memory, write_memory, extract_write_data, compute_seed_addrs)
  dispatch to CPU when addr_nets is None
- train.rs: engine.finalize_phase_b() called unconditionally before Phase C

RwLock trie (Phase 2b):
- trie.rs: Mutex<HierarchicalTrie> → RwLock<HierarchicalTrie>
  write_batch/reset use write(), read_batch/total_nodes/total_entries use read()
  Multiple concurrent reads now possible (prefetch + main reads overlap)

Proactive prefetch (Phase 2b):
- engine.rs: spawn_proactive_prefetch(h_mean) — dispatches BOTH address computation
  AND trie read to a single background thread (CpuAddrBank + RwLock read)
- train.rs Phase C: switched from compute_seed_addrs + spawn_prefetch_read to
  spawn_proactive_prefetch — GPU is free from the moment h_mean is transferred

Lifecycle after this change:
  Phase B  → GPU AddrNets trained, all addr computation on GPU
  finalize → weights synced to CpuAddrBank, GPU tensors freed
  Phase C  → CpuAddrBank drives all addr computation, trie reads concurrent

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Auto-resolve promises in TypeScript codegen to prevent silent RPC drops (#15901)

When users write un-awaited fluent chains like:
  const db = builder.addPostgres('pg').addDatabase('db');
  builder.addContainer('c', 'nginx').withReference(db);
  await builder.build().run();

the generated TypeScript now accepts PromiseLike<T> for handle parameters
(via Awaitable<T>) and automatically resolves them before RPC calls.
build() flushes all pending un-awaited promises before proceeding.

Key design decisions in transport.ts:
- trackPromise: .then(resolve, reject) removes from set on settlement.
  Reject handler swallows errors to prevent Node.js unhandled-rejection
  crashes. Errors are NOT eagerly collected — only promises still pending
  at flush time are observed, so user-caught rejections are not re-thrown.
- flushPendingPromises: snapshots the pending set before awaiting to
  prevent deadlock. The build promise is tracked by the PromiseImpl
  constructor while flush is suspended — without the snapshot, a while-loop
  re-check would find and await it, causing a circular await.
- Promise.allSettled collects all errors as AggregateError.

Testing:
- 8 vitest unit tests for trackPromise/flushPendingPromises covering
  deadlock prevention, error aggregation, and user-caught rejection safety
- E2E test with aspire start/stop validating runtime behavior
- 73 snapshot tests for generated code shape

Fixes #15899

* Remove unnecessary #include <stddef.h> that breaks MSVC CI

FlashCpp provides offsetof as a builtin, so the include is not needed.
On MSVC, stddef.h pulls in vcruntime.h which FlashCpp's lexer can't
resolve, causing a compile failure.

* Fix baseSubobjectSizeInBytes() returning 1 instead of 0 for empty structs

The fallback path was calling sizeInBytes() which applies
enforceMinimumCompleteObjectSize (bumping 0 → 1 for complete objects).
Base subobjects should NOT get this bump per the Itanium ABI — empty
bases have size 0 as base subobjects. Use currentLayoutOffset() directly
instead, which returns the raw layout size without the minimum-size
enforcement.

* Propagate is_no_unique_address in lazy template nested struct instantiation path

cr(CR-001): add time-aware rate constraint and acceptance criterion

The pre-resolved annualRate pattern supports historical recalculations
only if the caller uses calculationDate (not LocalDate.now()) as the
effectiveDate for BusinessConfigService.getDecimalValue(). Making this
constraint explicit prevents a silent correctness bug in petition039

commit.message:
fix(blender): add asyncio.Lock to serialize concurrent socket calls

Root cause: viewport_broadcast_loop (every 3s) + /api/preview both call
blender.call_tool() concurrently on the same TCP socket. Without a lock,
write/read operations interleave → one caller consumes the other's response
→ the other hangs for 30s (timeout) → returns 1x1 placeholder PNG.

Fix: asyncio.Lock in BlenderSocketAdapter.send_command() ensures only
one coroutine uses the socket at a time. Others queue without blocking
the event loop.

commit.message:
Document sort order controls for heatmap axes in Lens (#5814)

Kibana PR #244696 added user-configurable sort order controls for
heatmap visualization axes in Lens (Stack 9.4, Serverless). The Style
settings docs for both axes had no mention of this new dropdown.

### Changes

- **Style settings > Vertical axis**: Added **Sort order** setting
(Unsorted/Ascending/Descending) with `{applies_to}` tags for
`serverless: ga` and `stack: ga 9.4`
- **Style settings > Horizontal axis**: Same, with note that sort order
is unavailable for time-based axes (disabled in UI for date histogram)
- **Best practices**: Updated "Order categories meaningfully" tip to
reference the Sort order style setting

Option labels and behavior match the Kibana source (`sort_order.tsx`):
`Unsorted` maps to undefined/auto-detection, `Ascending`/`Descending`
auto-detect numeric vs. alphabetical sorting based on column type.

docs: fix and complete API Reference with all actual endpoints

Replaced inaccurate API Reference with comprehensive table of all endpoints:

Endpoints now documented:
- /get/schema - Get command schema
- /get/version - Get build version
- /get/status - Get server status
- /get/token - Get security token
- /post/run - Execute command
- /get/global - Get entire global JSON
- /get/global/path - Get value at JSON path
- /post/global - Replace entire global JSON (requires token)
- /post/global/patch - Apply merge patch
- /post/global/persist - Force save to disk
- / - Serve web GUI
- /* OPTIONS - CORS preflight

Added accurate documentation for:
- Authentication requirements (token for certain endpoints)
- Query parameters (e.g., ?path=/a/b)
- Request body formats
- Response structures
- Error codes with HTTP status mappings
- curl examples for each endpoint
